examples.label: Post-signature obligation plan for a signed SaaS MSA

input.user_message: We just signed this SaaS deal. Build me a post-signature tracking plan.
input.contract_excerpt: Effective Date: 2026-06-01. Term: 24 months ending 2028-05-31. Auto-renews for 12 months unless either party gives 60 days' written notice. Annual fee USD 240,000, invoiced annually in advance, due net 30. Provider shall maintain 99.9% monthly uptime, with 5% service credit per 0.1% miss, reported in the monthly invoice. Provider shall provide a quarterly security report (SOC 2 status + incidents) by the 15th of the month following quarter-end. Customer shall give Provider written notice within 5 business days of any suspected data breach. Customer has audit rights once per year on 30 days' notice.

obligations.obligation_text: Pay annual subscription fee of USD 240,000 in advance, net 30
obligations.quoted_text: Annual fee USD 240,000, invoiced annually in advance, due net 30.

actions.trigger: Annually starting Effective Date + invoice receipt
actions.payload: Process USD 240,000 invoice from Provider; verify amount; approve for payment
Track Provider's reported uptime against 99.9% threshold; flag any miss

obligations.obligation_text: Provide 5% service credit per 0.1% SLA miss in the following monthly invoice
obligations.quoted_text: 5% service credit per 0.1% miss, reported in the monthly invoice

actions.payload: Verify Provider applies correct service credit on next invoice; reconcile

obligations.risk_note: Customer must independently verify credits are applied — Providers commonly under-credit unless flagged.

obligations.obligation_text: Provide quarterly security report (SOC 2 status + incidents) by the 15th of the month following quarter-end
obligations.quoted_text: Provider shall provide a quarterly security report (SOC 2 status + incidents) by the 15th of the month following quarter-end

actions.payload: Confirm receipt of Provider's quarterly security report; review SOC 2 status and incident list
Provider quarterly security report due in 14 days — confirm Provider has it on calendar

obligations.risk_note: Vendor-delivered reports are commonly late — proactive nudge at T-14 reduces miss rate.

obligations.obligation_text: Give Provider written notice within 5 business days of any suspected data breach
obligations.quoted_text: Customer shall give Provider written notice within 5 business days of any suspected data breach

actions.payload: On any suspected breach, notify Provider in writing within 5 business days; log notification

obligations.risk_note: Short notice window — must be wired into Customer's incident response playbook, not relied on as ad-hoc.

obligations.obligation_text: Audit Provider once per year on 30 days' notice (optional right)
obligations.quoted_text: Customer has audit rights once per year on 30 days' notice

actions.trigger: Annual review window (e.g., Q1 each year)
actions.payload: Decide whether to exercise annual audit right; if yes, send 30-day notice to Provider

renewal_triggers.action_required: Decide renewal vs non-renewal vs renegotiate; if non-renew, deliver written notice to Provider by 2028-04-01.

summary: SaaS MSA between Customer and Provider, USD 240k/year, term 2026-06-01 to 2028-05-31, auto-renewing for 12 months.
6 obligations extracted: 2 recurring payment/reporting, 2 event-triggered (SLA credit, breach notice), 1 conditional audit right, 1 SLA-monitored.
Highest-risk: OBL-005 breach notification (5 business days) — wire into incident response; OBL-003 service-credit reconciliation — verify each invoice.
Critical renewal date: notice deadline 2028-04-01 (60 days before auto-renew). Alerts at T-90/60/30.

Recommended cadence: monthly SLA review (operations), quarterly security report intake (infosec), annual audit decision (infosec), annual invoice processing (finance), renewal decision (procurement, owned at T-90 days).
